name: Monte Padrão Loop from Santo Tirso
name_original: Santo Tirso, passando pelas Quedas de Fervença — percurso circular
description: Cycle a moderate 10.1-mile (16.3 km) circular route in Santo Tirso, passing the Fervença Falls and historic São Bento Monastery.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Santo Tirso traverse diverse landscapes within the Porto Metropolitan Area of Portugal. The region features green landscapes along the Ave River, offering a mix of terrain including asphalt, paved sections, cobblestones, and some unpaved segments. Elevation gains vary across the area, from gentle paths to more challenging climbs, with the highest point reaching 527 meters at Alto de S. Jorge. Best touring cycling routes around Santo Tirso The most popular touring cycling route is Monte Padrão Loop from Santo Tirso, a 10.1 miles (16.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes by Fervença Falls and the historic São Bento Monastery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alvito Bridge – Road Parallel to the Leça River loop from Santo Tirso, a moderate 27.1 miles (43.7 km) path. This longer route offers varied terrain along the Leça River. Local touring cyclists also love the Monte da Senhora da Assunção – View of Serra de Agrela loop from Antiga Estação de Santo Tirso, a 25.1 miles (40.4 km) trail leading through areas with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Touring cycling around Santo Tirso is defined by green river valleys, varied terrain including paved and unpaved sections, and climbs to viewpoints like Monte da Senhora da Assunção. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with substantial elevation. The routes in Santo Tirso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santo Tirso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Santo Tirso?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Santo Tirso, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Santo Tirso?
A: The routes in Santo Tirso feature diverse terrain, including asphalt, paved sections, cobblestones, and some unpaved segments. This mix provides a varied cycling experience, from smooth rides to more challenging surfaces.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes with significant elevation gain for a challenge?
A: Yes, Santo Tirso offers routes with substantial elevation gain for experienced cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23732991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de São Miguel-o-Anjo – View of Serra de Agrela loop from Santo Tirso&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging ride covering 52.9 km with nearly 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Which touring cycling routes are circular in Santo Tirso?
A: Many routes in Santo Tirso are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379981779/monte-padrao-loop-from-santo-tirso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Padrão Loop from Santo Tirso&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.3 km long and passes by notable landmarks.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes in Santo Tirso?
A: You can discover several natural attractions while cycling in Santo Tirso. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379981779/monte-padrao-loop-from-santo-tirso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Padrão Loop from Santo Tirso&lt;/a&gt; passes by the refreshing Fervença Falls. The Ave River and its rehabilitated margins also offer scenic views and leisure opportunities along many routes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by touring bike in Santo Tirso?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379981779/monte-padrao-loop-from-santo-tirso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Padrão Loop from Santo Tirso&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/944875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint Benedict&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the region features the ancient Monte Padrão Hillfort and the Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Assunção, which offers panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Santo Tirso?
A: Santo Tirso's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Warm to hot summers and mild, very wet winters mean spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with moderate temperatures and less rainfall.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Santo Tirso?
A: While specific 'easy' routes are not highlighted in the data, the region offers diverse paths. The municipal cycle path integrated into the Ave River pedestrian walkway provides a safer and more accessible option for families. Always check route details for terrain and elevation to ensure it suits your family's abilities.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Santo Tirso?
A: The komoot community rates touring cycling in Santo Tirso highly, with an average of 4.4 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of paved and unpaved sections, and the opportunities to discover both natural beauty like waterfalls and historical sites such as the São Bento Monastery.

Q: Are there any longer touring cycling routes for extended rides?
A: Yes, for longer rides, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32224441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alvito Bridge – Road Parallel to the Leça River loop from Santo Tirso&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route covers 43.7 km and offers varied terrain along the Leça River, making for a substantial day out.

Q: Are there viewpoints along the touring cycling routes in Santo Tirso?
A: Yes, several routes lead to impressive viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17500881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte da Senhora da Assunção – View of Serra de Agrela loop from Antiga Estação de Santo Tirso&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that rewards cyclists with panoramic views from Monte da Senhora da Assunção, overlooking Santo Tirso, the Ave Valley, and even the Atlantic Ocean on clear days.

Q: What is the highest point in the Santo Tirso municipality that can be reached by bike?
A: The highest point in the municipality is Alto de S. Jorge, reaching 527 meters of altitude. While not all routes directly ascend to it, many challenging touring cycling routes in the region will take you through areas with significant elevation, offering similar high-point views.
tips: The Monastery of São Bento, also known as the Monastery of Santo Tirso, is located on the left bank of the Ave River, in the city of Santo Tirso, Portugal. Founded in 978, it belonged to the Benedictine Order and was one of the most powerful monasteries in Portugal. Currently, it is classified as a National Monument.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Santo Tirso, passing by the Fervença Falls — circular route is classified as a moderate touring bicycle ride. While it requires a good fitness level, its predominantly paved surfaces make it generally accessible for various skill levels. Expect an elevation gain and loss of around 230 meters over its 16.3-kilometer length.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly paved surfaces, including approximately 8.38 km of asphalt and 5.23 km of paved sections. There are also about 2.22 km of cobblestones and a small unpaved portion of 0.417 km. This mix provides a varied cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main attractions or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight of this circular route is its passage by the stunning Fervença Waterfalls. Another significant point of interest is the historic Monastery of Saint Benedict, Santo Tirso, a national monument known for its Romanesque architecture.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Cyclists can expect to complete the 16.3-kilometer route in approximately 1 hour and 5 minutes, though this can vary based on your pace and stops to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists or families?
faqs.answer: While categorized as moderate and requiring good fitness, the route is generally considered suitable for all skill levels due to its mostly paved terrain. However, the 230 meters of elevation gain and loss mean some effort is required, so beginners should be prepared for some climbs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Porto District, where this route is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures for outdoor activities, allowing you to fully enjoy the natural beauty and cultural richness.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the exact trailhead is not detailed, but as the route is circular and passes through Santo Tirso, parking options would typically be available within or near the town center, or at points close to the main attractions like the Monastery or Fervença Falls.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily for touring bicycles, if you plan to bring a dog, it's generally advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in more populated areas or near road sections. Given the mixed terrain, ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling alongside you and the surfaces encountered.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Santo Tirso, passing by the Fervença Falls — circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. You will find sections that overlap with the PR2 STS Padrão, PR 8 STS - Quintas e Parques, PR 6 STS - Vale do Leça - Linear, Histórico Pré-Industrial, PR 4 STS - Abraço, Avenida Comendador Manuel Almeida, and Rua das Almas.

waypoints.name: Monastery of Saint Benedict, Santo Tirso
